notes: Wave 12 Track A candidate, from country-hub-data.js gap scan.

Date rule (fixed, Dec 25) confirmed via WebSearch against canada.ca and Wikipedia; Christmas Day is a statutory holiday in every province/territory, unlike Boxing Day which is only mandatory in Ontario + federal sector — cross-checked against the already-live boxing-day-canada event content for consistency, no contradiction.

Competitor check: trip.com/arabnews24.ca mention it only inside general holiday-season roundups, ar.immigrant.today covers cultural traditions only with no legal/statutory detail, no Arabic competitor draws the Christmas-vs-Boxing-Day legal distinction or a dedicated countdown.

Content-policy check: this is observance/legal-status content (a federal/provincial statutory holiday and its commercial/social traditions), not an affirmation of Christian theological claims — consistent with the owner's 2026-07-14 standing rule (which targeted Resurrection/Easter theological content specifically) and consistent with the already-live christmas-usa/christmas-germany/christmas-jordan/christmas-palestine pages.
